82	# If a request has a matched prefix length (within the waiting queue) larger than this value,
83	# the scheduler deprioritizes this request
84	IN_BATCH_PREFIX_CACHING_DEPRIORITIZE_THRESHOLD = int(
85	    os.environ.get("IN_BATCH_PREFIX_CACHING_DEPRIORITIZE_THRESHOLD", "32")
86	)
87	
88	
89	IGNORE_EOS_RESERVE_TOKENS = 1
90	
91	
92	def match_prefix_for_req(
93	    tree_cache: BasePrefixCache,
94	    req: Req,
95	    token_ids: Optional[array[int]] = None,
96	    *,
97	    cow_mamba: bool = False,
98	    include_req: bool = False,
99	):
100	    if token_ids is None:
101	        token_ids = req.origin_input_ids + req.output_ids
102	
103	    # unified_kv SWA lives in a per-request ring that's not content-stable and is
104	    # never stored in the radix tree, so a reused prefix carries stale SWA. Cap
105	    # the match by the trailing sliding window so it gets re-prefilled, rewriting
106	    # this request's SWA ring. No-op for other layouts.
107	    reprefill_tail = tree_cache.swa_reprefill_tail_tokens()
108	    key_limit = max(0, len(token_ids) - reprefill_tail) if reprefill_tail else None
109	
110	    match_result = tree_cache.match_prefix(
111	        MatchPrefixParams(
112	            key=RadixKey(token_ids=token_ids, extra_key=req.extra_key, limit=key_limit),
113	            cow_mamba=cow_mamba,
114	            req=req if include_req else None,
115	        )
116	    )
117	    if envs.SGLANG_RADIX_FORCE_MISS.get():
118	        match_result = zero_match_result(tree_cache, match_result)
119	    (
120	        req.prefix_indices,
121	        req.last_node,
122	        req.last_host_node,
123	        req.best_match_node,
124	        req.host_hit_length,
125	        req.swa_host_hit_length,
126	        req.mamba_host_hit_length,
127	    ) = (
128	        match_result.device_indices,
129	        match_result.last_device_node,
130	        match_result.last_host_node,
131	        match_result.best_match_node,
132	        match_result.host_hit_length,
133	        match_result.swa_host_hit_length,
134	        match_result.mamba_host_hit_length,
135	    )
136	    max_len = req._compute_max_prefix_len(len(token_ids))
137	    req.num_matched_prefix_tokens = min(
138	        len(req.prefix_indices) + req.host_hit_length, max_len
139	    )
140	    if match_result.mamba_branching_seqlen is not None:
141	        req.mamba_branching_seqlen = match_result.mamba_branching_seqlen
142	    if match_result.cache_protected_len is not None:
143	        req.cache_protected_len = match_result.cache_protected_len
144	    return match_result
145	
146	
147	class CacheAwarePolicy(Enum):
148	    """Scheduling policies that are aware of the tree cache."""
149	
150	    LPM = "lpm"  # longest prefix match
151	    DFS_WEIGHT = "dfs-weight"  # depth-first search weighting
152	
153	
154	class CacheAgnosticPolicy(Enum):
155	    """Scheduling policies that are not aware of the tree cache."""
156	
157	    FCFS = "fcfs"  # first come first serve
158	    LOF = "lof"  # longest output first
159	    RANDOM = "random"
160	    ROUTING_KEY = "routing-key"  # prioritize by routing key frequency in running batch
161	
162	
163	class SchedulePolicy:
164	    Policy = Union[CacheAwarePolicy, CacheAgnosticPolicy]
165	
166	    def __init__(
167	        self,
168	        policy: str,
169	        tree_cache: BasePrefixCache,
170	        enable_hierarchical_cache: bool,
171	        enable_priority_scheduling: bool,
172	        schedule_low_priority_values_first: bool,
173	    ):
174	        self.policy = self._validate_and_adjust_policy(policy, tree_cache)
175	        self.tree_cache = tree_cache
176	        self.enable_hierarchical_cache = enable_hierarchical_cache
177	        self.enable_priority_scheduling = enable_priority_scheduling
178	        self.schedule_low_priority_values_first = schedule_low_priority_values_first
179	        self.priority_sign = 1 if schedule_low_priority_values_first else -1
180	
181	        # It is used to find the matching prefix for in-batch prefix caching.
182	        self.waiting_queue_radix_tree = RadixCache.create_simulated()
183	
184	    def calc_priority(
185	        self, waiting_queue: List[Req], running_batch: Optional[ScheduleBatch] = None
186	    ) -> None:
187	        policy = self._determine_active_policy(waiting_queue)
188	
189	        # Populate req.num_matched_prefix_tokens at schedule time. Cache-aware policies
190	        # set it in _compute_prefix_matches; do the same full match for
191	        # cache-agnostic policies when the radix supports it, so the load
192	        # snapshot has it. Skip on decode (never prefills).
193	        if (
194	            not isinstance(policy, CacheAwarePolicy)
195	            and self.tree_cache.supports_fast_match_prefix()
196	            and get_server_args().disaggregation_mode != "decode"
197	        ):
198	            for r in waiting_queue:
199	                match_prefix_for_req(self.tree_cache, r, include_req=True)
200	
201	        if self.policy == CacheAgnosticPolicy.FCFS:
202	            if self.enable_priority_scheduling:
203	                SchedulePolicy._sort_by_priority_and_fcfs(
204	                    waiting_queue, self.priority_sign
205	                )
206	            return
207	
208	        if isinstance(policy, CacheAwarePolicy):
209	            temporary_deprioritized = self._compute_prefix_matches(
210	                waiting_queue, policy
211	            )
212	            if policy == CacheAwarePolicy.LPM:
213	                SchedulePolicy._sort_by_longest_prefix(
214	                    waiting_queue, temporary_deprioritized
215	                )
216	            elif policy == CacheAwarePolicy.DFS_WEIGHT:
217	                SchedulePolicy._sort_by_dfs_weight(waiting_queue, self.tree_cache)
218	            else:
219	                raise ValueError(f"Unknown CacheAware Policy: {policy=}")
220	        else:
221	            if policy == CacheAgnosticPolicy.FCFS:
222	                pass
223	            elif policy == CacheAgnosticPolicy.LOF:
224	                SchedulePolicy._sort_by_longest_output(
225	                    waiting_queue,
226	                    self.enable_priority_scheduling,
227	                    self.priority_sign,
228	                )
229	            elif policy == CacheAgnosticPolicy.RANDOM:
230	                SchedulePolicy._sort_randomly(waiting_queue)
231	            elif policy == CacheAgnosticPolicy.ROUTING_KEY:
232	                if running_batch is not None:
233	                    SchedulePolicy._sort_by_routing_key(waiting_queue, running_batch)
234	            else:
235	                raise ValueError(f"Unknown CacheAgnostic Policy: {policy=}")
236	
237	    def _determine_active_policy(self, waiting_queue: List[Req]) -> Policy:
238	        if self.policy == CacheAwarePolicy.LPM and len(waiting_queue) > 128:
239	            # Turn off the expensive prefix matching and sorting when the #queue is large.
240	            return CacheAgnosticPolicy.FCFS
241	        return self.policy
242	
243	    def _validate_and_adjust_policy(
244	        self, policy: str, tree_cache: BasePrefixCache
245	    ) -> Policy:
246	        """
247	        Validates the policy and adjusts it if necessary based on tree cache settings.
248	        """
249	        try:
250	            policy_enum = CacheAwarePolicy(policy)
251	            if getattr(tree_cache, "disable", True):
252	                # If tree_cache is disabled, using CacheAgnosticPolicy policy
253	                return CacheAgnosticPolicy.FCFS
254	            return policy_enum
255	        except ValueError:
256	            try:
257	                return CacheAgnosticPolicy(policy)
258	            except ValueError:
259	                raise ValueError(f"Unknown schedule_policy: {policy=}")
260	
261	    def _compute_prefix_matches(
262	        self, waiting_queue: List[Req], policy: CacheAwarePolicy
263	    ) -> Set[int]:
264	        """
265	        Computes and caches the matching prefixes for requests in the waiting queue,
266	            and handles in-batch prefix caching logic.
267	        """
268	        temporary_deprioritized: Set[int] = set()
269	        self.waiting_queue_radix_tree.reset()
270	
271	        for r in waiting_queue:
272	            prefix_ids = r.origin_input_ids + r.output_ids
273	            extra_key = r.extra_key
274	            match_result = match_prefix_for_req(
275	                self.tree_cache, r, prefix_ids, include_req=True
276	            )
277	
278	            # NOTE(sang): This logic is for in-batch prefix caching;
279	            # If there are more than 1 request that have small matching prefix from
280	            # existing cache, but all those requests share the same prefix, we prefer
281	            # to schedule only one of them so that we can increase the cache hit rate.
282	            # We prefer to set IN_BATCH_PREFIX_CACHING_CHECK_THRESHOLD > 0 because too small
283	            # threshold means we cannot use in-batch prefix caching for short prefixes.
284	            # It is kind of common when the engine is long running (e.g., imagine the prefix "the").
285	            if len(r.prefix_indices) <= IN_BATCH_PREFIX_CACHING_CHECK_THRESHOLD:
286	                match_result = self.waiting_queue_radix_tree.match_prefix(
287	                    MatchPrefixParams(
288	                        key=RadixKey(token_ids=prefix_ids, extra_key=extra_key)
289	                    )
290	                )
291	                if envs.SGLANG_RADIX_FORCE_MISS.get():
292	                    match_result = zero_match_result(
293	                        self.waiting_queue_radix_tree, match_result
294	                    )
295	                in_batch_matching_prefixes = match_result.device_indices
296	                if (
297	                    len(in_batch_matching_prefixes)
298	                    >= IN_BATCH_PREFIX_CACHING_DEPRIORITIZE_THRESHOLD
299	                ):
300	                    temporary_deprioritized.add(r.rid)
301	                else:
302	                    # Insert with a dummy key
303	                    self.waiting_queue_radix_tree.insert(
304	                        InsertParams(
305	                            key=RadixKey(token_ids=prefix_ids, extra_key=extra_key),
306	                            value=torch.empty(len(prefix_ids), dtype=torch.bool),
307	                        )
308	                    )
309	        return temporary_deprioritized
310	
311	    @staticmethod
312	    def _sort_by_longest_prefix(
313	        waiting_queue: List[Req], temporary_deprioritized: Set[int]
314	    ) -> None:
315	        """Sorts the waiting queue based on the longest prefix match."""
316	        waiting_queue.sort(
317	            key=lambda r: (
318	                -r.num_matched_prefix_tokens
319	                if r.rid not in temporary_deprioritized
320	                else float("inf")
321	            )
322	        )
323	
324	    @staticmethod
325	    def _sort_by_dfs_weight(
326	        waiting_queue: List[Req], tree_cache: BasePrefixCache
327	    ) -> None:
328	        """Sorts the waiting queue based on a depth-first search weighting."""
329	        last_node_to_reqs = defaultdict(list)
330	        for req in waiting_queue:
331	            last_node_to_reqs[req.last_node].append(req)
332	
333	        node_to_weight = defaultdict(int)
334	        for node in last_node_to_reqs:
335	            node_to_weight[node] = len(last_node_to_reqs[node])
336	        SchedulePolicy._calc_weight(tree_cache.root_node, node_to_weight)
337	
338	        waiting_queue.clear()
339	        SchedulePolicy._get_dfs_priority(
340	            tree_cache.root_node,
341	            node_to_weight,
342	            last_node_to_reqs,
343	            waiting_queue,
344	        )
345	
346	    @staticmethod
347	    def _sort_by_longest_output(
348	        waiting_queue: List[Req],
349	        enable_priority_scheduling: bool,
350	        priority_sign: int,
351	    ) -> None:
352	        """Sorts the waiting queue based on the longest output (max_new_tokens). If using priority scheduling, sort by priority first."""
353	        if enable_priority_scheduling:
354	            waiting_queue.sort(
355	                key=lambda x: (
356	                    x.priority * priority_sign,
357	                    -x.sampling_params.max_new_tokens,
358	                )
359	            )
360	        else:
361	            waiting_queue.sort(key=lambda x: -x.sampling_params.max_new_tokens)
362	
363	    @staticmethod
364	    def _sort_randomly(waiting_queue: List[Req]) -> None:
365	        """Shuffles the waiting queue randomly."""
366	        random.shuffle(waiting_queue)
367	
368	    @staticmethod
369	    def _sort_by_priority_and_fcfs(
370	        waiting_queue: List[Req], priority_sign: int
371	    ) -> None:
372	        """Sorts the waiting queue based on the request priority then received titmestamp."""
373	        waiting_queue.sort(
374	            key=lambda x: (
375	                x.priority * priority_sign,
376	                x.time_stats.wait_queue_entry_time,
377	            )
378	        )
379	
380	    @staticmethod
381	    def _sort_by_routing_key(
382	        waiting_queue: List[Req], running_batch: ScheduleBatch
383	    ) -> None:
384	        """Sorts waiting queue by routing key frequency in running batch."""
385	        routing_key_counts = Counter(
386	            r.routing_key for r in running_batch.reqs if r.routing_key
387	        )
388	
389	        if _ROUTING_KEY_POLICY_DEBUG_LOG:
390	            waiting_keys_before = [r.routing_key for r in waiting_queue]
391	            logger.info(
392	                f"routing_key_counts={dict(routing_key_counts)}, "
393	                f"waiting_keys_before={waiting_keys_before}"
394	            )
395	
396	        if not routing_key_counts:
397	            return
398	
399	        def sort_key(req: Req):
400	            key = req.routing_key
401	            if key and key in routing_key_counts:
402	                count = routing_key_counts[key]
403	                return (0, -count, key)
404	            else:
405	                return (1, 0, key or "")
406	
407	        waiting_queue.sort(key=sort_key)
408	
409	        if _ROUTING_KEY_POLICY_DEBUG_LOG:
410	            waiting_keys_after = [r.routing_key for r in waiting_queue]
411	            logger.info(f"waiting_keys_after={waiting_keys_after}")
412	
413	    @staticmethod
414	    def _calc_weight(cur_node: TreeNode, node_to_weight: Dict[TreeNode, int]) -> None:
415	        for child in cur_node.children.values():
416	            SchedulePolicy._calc_weight(child, node_to_weight)
417	            node_to_weight[cur_node] += node_to_weight[child]
418	
419	    @staticmethod
420	    def _get_dfs_priority(
421	        cur_node: TreeNode,
422	        node_to_priority: Dict[TreeNode, int],
423	        last_node_to_reqs: Dict[TreeNode, List[Req]],
424	        q: List,
425	    ) -> None:
426	        children = [child for child in cur_node.children.values()]
427	        children.sort(key=lambda x: -node_to_priority[x])
428	        for child in children:
429	            SchedulePolicy._get_dfs_priority(
430	                child, node_to_priority, last_node_to_reqs, q
431	            )
432	        q.extend(last_node_to_reqs[cur_node])
433	
434	
435	class AddReqResult(Enum):
436	    CONTINUE = auto()  # Continue to add requests
437	    NO_TOKEN = auto()  # No token left
438	    OTHER = auto()  # Other reasons to stop adding requests
439	
440	
441	class PrefillAdder:
442	    def __init__(
443	        self,
444	        page_size: int,
445	        tree_cache: BasePrefixCache,
446	        token_to_kv_pool_allocator: BaseTokenToKVPoolAllocator,
447	        running_batch: ScheduleBatch,
448	        new_token_ratio: float,
449	        rem_input_tokens: int,
450	        rem_chunk_tokens: Optional[int],
451	        num_mixed_decode_tokens: int = 0,
452	        priority_scheduling_preemption_threshold: int = 0,
453	        max_prefill_bs: int = 0,
454	        max_running_requests: Optional[int] = None,
455	        prefill_max_requests: Optional[int] = None,
456	        prefill_delayer_single_pass: Optional[PrefillDelayerSinglePassExecutor] = None,
457	        dllm_config: Optional[DllmConfig] = None,
458	        waiting_queue_len: int = 0,
459	    ):
460	        self.page_size = page_size
461	        self.tree_cache = tree_cache
462	        self.token_to_kv_pool_allocator = token_to_kv_pool_allocator
463	        self.running_batch = running_batch
464	        self.new_token_ratio = new_token_ratio
465	        self.rem_input_tokens = rem_input_tokens - num_mixed_decode_tokens
466	        self.rem_chunk_tokens = rem_chunk_tokens
467	        self.dllm_config = dllm_config
468	
469	        if self.dllm_config is not None:
470	            self._init_dllm_meta(dllm_config)
471	
472	        if self.rem_chunk_tokens is not None:
473	            self.rem_chunk_tokens -= num_mixed_decode_tokens
474	        self.rem_total_token_offset = num_mixed_decode_tokens
475	        self.cur_rem_token_offset = num_mixed_decode_tokens
476	
477	        self.req_states = None
478	        self.can_run_list = []
479	        self.preempt_list = []
480	        self.new_chunked_req = None
481	        self.log_hit_tokens = 0
482	        self.reprocessed_log_hit_tokens = 0
483	        # TODO(lsyin): report the real input tokens excluding page alignment
484	        self.log_input_tokens = 0
485	        self.reprocessed_log_input_tokens = 0
486	
487	        if running_batch is not None:
488	            # Estimate the offset in the remaining token space
489	            self.rem_total_token_offset += sum(
490	                [
491	                    self._get_running_request_total_token_offset(r)
492	                    for r in running_batch.reqs
493	                ]
494	            )
495	
496	        # DeepSeek V4 HiSparse wraps an SWATokenToKVPoolAllocator internally and
497	        # exposes the full SWA allocator interface.
498	        self.is_hybrid_swa = isinstance(
499	            self.token_to_kv_pool_allocator,
500	            (SWATokenToKVPoolAllocator, DeepSeekV4HiSparseTokenToKVPoolAllocator),
501	        )
502	        self.is_all_swa = isinstance(
503	            self.token_to_kv_pool_allocator, PureSWATokenToKVPoolAllocator
504	        )
505	        self.is_hybrid_ssm_cache = self.tree_cache.supports_mamba()
506	
507	        self.rem_swa_token_offset = 0
508	
509	        # Unified-pool joint budget: a new mamba state consumes shared-gap bytes
510	        # that `rem_total_tokens` (full KV) otherwise counts as free, so reserve
511	        # the gap per new mamba slot or admission over-commits. Gate on the
512	        # ALLOCATOR being the unified Mamba composite, NOT on `is_hybrid_ssm_cache`
513	        # (False for `ChunkCache`, which would skip the reservation on the
514	        # chunk-cache path): the gap coupling is a property of the byte buffer.
515	        self._mamba_slot_cost = 0
516	        if isinstance(
517	            self.token_to_kv_pool_allocator, UnifiedMambaTokenToKVPoolAllocator
518	        ):
519	            self._mamba_slot_cost = (
520	                self.token_to_kv_pool_allocator.mamba_slot_full_token_cost()
521	            )
522	
523	        # `mamba_gap_reserve` is charged to `rem_total_tokens`, which INCLUDES
524	        # `full_evictable_size()` — but `alloc_req_slots` can only recover
525	        # MAMBA-recoverable bytes for a mamba slot (shared gap + peer holes +
526	        # mamba-evictable radix), NOT full-evictable. Gate new mamba slots on
527	        # that mamba-recoverable budget separately or an over-admit hits the
528	        # fail-loud `RuntimeError`. `None` outside the unified Mamba pool.
529	        self.rem_mamba_slots = None
530	        if self._mamba_slot_cost:
531	            self.rem_mamba_slots = (
532	                self.token_to_kv_pool_allocator.mamba_allocator.schedulable_available_size()
533	            )
534	            if self.is_hybrid_ssm_cache:
535	                self.rem_mamba_slots += self.tree_cache.mamba_evictable_size()
536	
537	        self.priority_scheduling_preemption_threshold = (
538	            priority_scheduling_preemption_threshold
539	        )
540	        self.dsa_prefill_cp_in_seq_split = is_dsa_prefill_cp_in_seq_split()
541	        self.max_running_requests = max_running_requests
542	        self.prefill_context_parallel_enabled = is_prefill_context_parallel_enabled()
543	        self.prefill_max_requests = prefill_max_requests
544	        self.prefill_delayer_single_pass = prefill_delayer_single_pass
545	        self.max_prefill_bs = max_prefill_bs
546	        # Snapshot of scheduler waiting_queue length at the start of this
547	        # prefill pass. Used by PrefillDelayer's queue-based trigger.
548	        self.waiting_queue_len = waiting_queue_len
549	
550	    def _init_dllm_meta(self, dllm_config: DllmConfig):
551	        self.dllm_block_size = dllm_config.block_size
552	        max_running_reqs = dllm_config.max_running_requests
553	
554	        self.rem_dllm_tokens = max_running_reqs * self.dllm_block_size
555	
556	    def _get_running_request_total_token_offset(self, req: Req) -> int:
557	        return (
558	            min(
559	                (req.sampling_params.max_new_tokens - len(req.output_ids)),
560	                CLIP_MAX_NEW_TOKENS,
561	            )
562	            * self.new_token_ratio
563	        )
564	
565	    @property
566	    def rem_total_tokens(self):
567	        if self.is_all_swa:
568	            available_and_evictable = (
569	                self.token_to_kv_pool_allocator.swa_available_size()
570	                + self.tree_cache.swa_evictable_size()
571	            )
572	        elif self.is_hybrid_swa:
573	            available_and_evictable = (
574	                self.token_to_kv_pool_allocator.full_available_size()
575	                + self.tree_cache.full_evictable_size()
576	            )
577	        elif self.is_hybrid_ssm_cache:
578	            available_and_evictable = (
579	                self.token_to_kv_pool_allocator.available_size()
580	                + self.tree_cache.full_evictable_size()
581	            )
582	        else:
583	            available_and_evictable = (
584	                self.token_to_kv_pool_allocator.available_size()
585	                + self.tree_cache.evictable_size()
586	            )
587	        return available_and_evictable - self.rem_total_token_offset
588	
589	    @property
590	    def rem_swa_tokens(self):
591	        return (
592	            self.token_to_kv_pool_allocator.swa_available_size()
593	            + self.tree_cache.swa_evictable_size()
594	            - self.rem_swa_token_offset
595	        )
596	
597	    @property
598	    def cur_rem_tokens(self):
599	        if self.is_all_swa:
600	            available_and_evictable = (
601	                self.token_to_kv_pool_allocator.swa_available_size()
602	                + self.tree_cache.swa_evictable_size()
603	            )
604	        elif self.is_hybrid_swa:
605	            available_and_evictable = (
606	                self.token_to_kv_pool_allocator.full_available_size()
607	                + self.tree_cache.full_evictable_size()
608	            )
609	        elif self.is_hybrid_ssm_cache:
610	            available_and_evictable = (
611	                self.token_to_kv_pool_allocator.available_size()
612	                + self.tree_cache.full_evictable_size()
613	            )
614	        else:
615	            available_and_evictable = (
616	                self.token_to_kv_pool_allocator.available_size()
617	                + self.tree_cache.evictable_size()
618	            )
619	
620	        return available_and_evictable - self.cur_rem_token_offset
621	
622	    def _swa_budget_for_req(
623	        self, extend_input_len: int, swa_host_hit_length: int = 0
624	    ) -> int:
625	        """SWA pool budget per request. Only valid when is_hybrid_swa is True.
626	
627	        With chunked prefill + overlap scheduler, the peak SWA occupancy is:
628	          chunk N (running, not yet in tree) + sliding window (locked in tree)
629	          + chunk N+1 (new allocation)
630	        Since chunk N and locked tokens are already excluded from
631	        swa_available + swa_evictable, the budget only needs to cover the
632	        chunk N+1 allocation. We floor at sliding_window_size to reserve
633	        room for the decode phase.
634	        """
635	        if self.rem_chunk_tokens is not None:
636	            alloc = min(extend_input_len, self.rem_chunk_tokens)
637	        else:
638	            alloc = extend_input_len
639	        window = self.tree_cache.sliding_window_size
640	        return max(alloc - window, 0) + self._swa_reserved_tokens(swa_host_hit_length)
641	
642	    def _swa_reserved_tokens(self, swa_host_hit_length: int = 0) -> int:
643	        """SWA tokens a request needs regardless of extend length: the sliding
644	        window (decode headroom) + allocator page slack + the load-back window
645	        charge. Shared floor of _swa_budget_for_req and _swa_chunk_cap."""
646	        reserved = self.tree_cache.sliding_window_size + self.page_size
647	        if swa_host_hit_length > 0:
648	            reserved += self.ceil_paged_tokens(swa_host_hit_length)
649	        return reserved
650	
651	    def _swa_chunk_cap(self, swa_host_hit_length: int = 0) -> int:
652	        """Largest page-aligned extend chunk the SWA pool can admit right now,
653	        keeping a sliding window of headroom below rem_swa_tokens; 0 if not
654	        even one page fits. Only valid when is_hybrid_swa is True.
655	
656	        Escape hatch for a request whose budget can never pass the
657	        _swa_budget_for_req gate (extend near/above the pool size, or a large
658	        load-back charge): without shrinking its chunk it would be rejected
659	        forever (head-of-line livelock). Shrinking is sound because past a
660	        chunk boundary only the sliding window stays locked — the rest turns
661	        evictable — so each pass's transient footprint fits the pool."""
662	        cap = int(self.rem_swa_tokens) - self._swa_reserved_tokens(swa_host_hit_length)
663	        if cap <= 0:
664	            return 0
665	        return cap // self.page_size * self.page_size
666	
667	    def _mamba_gap_budget_for_req(self, req: Req) -> int:
668	        """Shared-gap reservation (full-token-equivalents) for a request's new
669	        mamba state. Charged only on the SHARED Mamba pool (`_mamba_slot_cost > 0`)
670	        and only when the req has no state yet (`mamba_pool_idx is None`, mirroring
671	        `HybridReqToTokenPool.alloc`); 0 keeps baseline / SWA / non-Mamba unchanged.
672	
673	        Conservative by design (`_mamba_slot_cost` rounds UP). Does NOT reserve
674	        radix COW headroom or locked-but-evictable bytes — that residual is
675	        backstopped by the fail-loud RuntimeError in `alloc_req_slots`. FIXME: if
676	        over-admission crashes under pressure, make this more conservative (e.g.
677	        multiply by `MAMBA_STATE_PER_REQ_PREFIX_CACHE`)."""
678	        if self._mamba_slot_cost and req.mamba_pool_idx is None:
679	            return self._mamba_slot_cost
680	        return 0
681	
682	    def ceil_paged_tokens(self, tokens: int) -> int:
683	        return -(-tokens // self.page_size) * self.page_size
684	
685	    def budget_state(self):
686	        no_token = self.rem_total_tokens <= 0 or self.cur_rem_tokens <= 0
687	        if not no_token and self.is_hybrid_swa:
688	            no_token = self.rem_swa_tokens <= 0
689	        # Gate new mamba slots separately: rem_total_tokens' full_evictable can't
690	        # cover a mamba slot, which needs mamba-recoverable bytes (see __init__).
691	        if not no_token and self.rem_mamba_slots is not None:
692	            no_token = self.rem_mamba_slots <= 0
693	        if no_token:
694	            return AddReqResult.NO_TOKEN
695	
696	        if self.rem_input_tokens <= 0:
697	            return AddReqResult.OTHER
698	
699	        if self.dllm_config is not None:
700	            if self.rem_dllm_tokens <= 0:
701	                return AddReqResult.OTHER
702	        else:
703	            if self.rem_chunk_tokens is not None and self.rem_chunk_tokens <= 0:
704	                return AddReqResult.OTHER
705	
706	        return AddReqResult.CONTINUE
707	
708	    def _update_prefill_budget(
709	        self,
710	        prefix_len: int,
711	        extend_input_len: int,
712	        max_new_tokens: int,
713	        retracted_stain: bool,
714	        mamba_gap_reserve: int = 0,
715	    ):
716	        # TODO(lsyin): check this workaround logic, which only ensures the prefill will not out of memory, and may be too conservative
717	        extend_input_len = self.ceil_paged_tokens(extend_input_len)
718	
719	        # alloc_extend reserves an extra page_size per request to make sure the budget doesn't over-commit
720	        page_overhead = self.page_size
721	        # `mamba_gap_reserve` (shared Mamba pool only; 0 otherwise) charges the new
722	        # mamba state's shared-gap cost to BOTH full budgets: the slot is allocated
723	        # immediately (counts against `cur_rem`) and held for the request lifetime
724	        # (counts against `rem_total`). See `_mamba_gap_budget_for_req`.
725	        self.rem_total_token_offset += (
726	            extend_input_len + max_new_tokens + page_overhead + mamba_gap_reserve
727	        )
728	        self.cur_rem_token_offset += (
729	            extend_input_len + page_overhead + mamba_gap_reserve
730	        )
731	        # The new mamba slot also consumes one mamba-recoverable slot (gated
732	        # separately so full_evictable can't cover it — see __init__).
733	        if mamba_gap_reserve and self.rem_mamba_slots is not None:
734	            self.rem_mamba_slots -= 1
735	        self.rem_input_tokens -= extend_input_len
736	
737	        if self.is_hybrid_swa:
738	            self.rem_swa_token_offset += self._swa_budget_for_req(extend_input_len)
739	
740	        if self.dllm_config is not None:
741	            self.rem_dllm_tokens -= extend_input_len
742	        elif self.rem_chunk_tokens is not None:
743	            self.rem_chunk_tokens -= extend_input_len
744	
745	        # reprocessed_log_* is a subset of log_*; metrics_reporter subtracts it
746	        # when computing the first-attempt prefix cache hit rate.
747	        self.log_hit_tokens += prefix_len
748	        self.log_input_tokens += extend_input_len
749	        if retracted_stain:
750	            self.reprocessed_log_hit_tokens += prefix_len
751	            self.reprocessed_log_input_tokens += extend_input_len
752	
753	    def _get_dllm_remain_tokens(self) -> int:
754	        _rem_tokens = min(
755	            self.rem_dllm_tokens,
756	            self.dllm_block_size,
757	            int(self.rem_total_tokens),
758	        )
759	        if _rem_tokens <= 0:
760	            _rem_tokens = self.rem_dllm_tokens
761	
762	        return _rem_tokens
763	
764	    def _add_dllm_req(self, req: Req, prefix_len: int):
765	        # FIXME: consider the case when rem_dllm_tokens < dllm_block_size,
766	        # the diffusion unmask process may have some problems
767	        # Make sure at least one page is available
768	        trunc_len = (
769	            min(self.rem_dllm_tokens, self.dllm_block_size)
770	            // self.page_size
771	            * self.page_size
772	        )
773	
774	        req.set_extend_range(prefix_len, prefix_len + trunc_len)
775	
776	        self.can_run_list.append(req)
777	
778	        self._update_prefill_budget(
779	            prefix_len,
780	            trunc_len,
781	            0,
782	            req.retracted_stain,
783	            mamba_gap_reserve=self._mamba_gap_budget_for_req(req),
784	        )
785	
786	    def _req_inc_lock_ref(self, req: Req):
787	        result = self.tree_cache.inc_lock_ref(req.last_node)
788	        if self.is_hybrid_swa:
789	            req.swa_uuid_for_lock = result.swa_uuid_for_lock
790	
791	    def add_dllm_staging_req(self, req: Req):
792	        assert self.dllm_config is not None
793	        _rem_tokens = self._get_dllm_remain_tokens()
794	
795	        if _rem_tokens <= 0:
796	            return AddReqResult.NO_TOKEN
797	
798	        # Truncate input length to available tokens and update request metadata
799	        cand_extend_input_len = len(req.full_untruncated_fill_ids) - len(
800	            req.prefix_indices
801	        )
802	        if req.dllm_incomplete_ids and cand_extend_input_len > _rem_tokens:
803	            return AddReqResult.NO_TOKEN
804	        truncated = cand_extend_input_len > _rem_tokens
805	        new_len = min(cand_extend_input_len, _rem_tokens)
806	        req.set_extend_range(len(req.prefix_indices), len(req.prefix_indices) + new_len)
807	        self.can_run_list.append(req)
808	
809	        # Update budget: reserve max_new_tokens only if not truncated
810	        max_new_tokens = (
811	            min(req.sampling_params.max_new_tokens, CLIP_MAX_NEW_TOKENS)
812	            if not truncated
813	            else 0
814	        )
815	        self._update_prefill_budget(
816	            0,
817	            req.extend_range.length,
818	            max_new_tokens,
819	            req.retracted_stain,
820	            mamba_gap_reserve=self._mamba_gap_budget_for_req(req),
821	        )
822	
823	        # Return based on remaining token availability
824	        return (
825	            AddReqResult.NO_TOKEN
826	            if self._get_dllm_remain_tokens() <= 0
827	            else AddReqResult.CONTINUE
828	        )
829	
830	    def add_chunked_req(self, req: Req):
831	        if self.dllm_config is not None:
832	            _rem_tokens = self._get_dllm_remain_tokens()
833	        else:
834	            _rem_tokens = min(self.rem_chunk_tokens, int(self.rem_total_tokens))
835	            if self.is_hybrid_swa:
836	                # alloc_extend needs extend_num_tokens + page_size per request,
837	                # so reserve one page here to avoid OOM
838	                _rem_tokens = min(
839	                    _rem_tokens, int(self.rem_swa_tokens) - self.page_size
840	                )
841	            # The chunked_req must be added to the list; otherwise, it will cause a memory leak.
842	            # Therefore, in certain cases where _rem_tokens <= 0, it should be replaced with rem_chunk_tokens.
843	            if _rem_tokens <= 0:
844	                if self.is_hybrid_swa:
845	                    return req
846	                _rem_tokens = self.rem_chunk_tokens
847	
848	        # A mid-chunk rank prefills this pass regardless of the delayer
849	        # verdict, so report prefillable=True and ignore the result.
850	        if self.prefill_delayer_single_pass is not None:
851	            self.prefill_delayer_single_pass.negotiate_should_allow_prefill(
852	                local_prefillable=True,
853	                running_batch=self.running_batch.batch_size(),
854	                max_prefill_bs=self.max_prefill_bs,
855	                max_running_requests=self.max_running_requests,
856	                waiting_queue_len=self.waiting_queue_len,
857	            )
858	
859	        cand_extend_input_len = len(req.full_untruncated_fill_ids) - len(
860	            req.prefix_indices
861	        )
862	        truncated = cand_extend_input_len > _rem_tokens
863	        new_len = min(cand_extend_input_len, _rem_tokens)
864	        req.set_extend_range(len(req.prefix_indices), len(req.prefix_indices) + new_len)
865	        self.can_run_list.append(req)
866	        self._update_prefill_budget(
867	            0,
868	            req.extend_range.length,
869	            (
870	                min(req.sampling_params.max_new_tokens, CLIP_MAX_NEW_TOKENS)
871	                if not truncated
872	                else 0
873	            ),
874	            req.retracted_stain,
875	            mamba_gap_reserve=self._mamba_gap_budget_for_req(req),
876	        )
877	
878	        # Return if chunked prefill not finished
879	        return req if truncated else None
880	
881	    @contextmanager
882	    def _lock_node(self, last_node: TreeNode):
883	        dec_lock_params = None
884	        try:
885	            result = self.tree_cache.inc_lock_ref(last_node)
886	            if self.tree_cache.is_tree_cache():
887	                # init_load_back may revive SWA/Mamba tombstones while this
888	                # temporary admission lock is held. Release must mirror the
889	                # exact nodes skipped at acquire time.
890	                dec_lock_params = result.to_dec_params()
891	            yield None
892	        finally:
893	            if dec_lock_params is not None:
894	                self.tree_cache.dec_lock_ref(last_node, dec_lock_params)
895	            else:
896	                self.tree_cache.dec_lock_ref(last_node)
897	
898	    def add_one_req_ignore_eos(self, req: Req):
899	        cand_extend_input_len = len(req.full_untruncated_fill_ids) - len(
900	            req.prefix_indices
901	        )
902	        paged_input = self.ceil_paged_tokens(cand_extend_input_len)
903	        # Shared Mamba pool: fold the new mamba state's shared-gap cost into the
904	        # budget gate so admission can't over-commit (0 for baseline / non-Mamba).
905	        paged_input += self._mamba_gap_budget_for_req(req)
906	        if paged_input > min(self.cur_rem_tokens, self.rem_total_tokens):
907	            return AddReqResult.NO_TOKEN
908	        if self.is_hybrid_swa:
909	            if self._swa_budget_for_req(cand_extend_input_len) > self.rem_swa_tokens:
910	                return AddReqResult.NO_TOKEN
911	
912	        def add_req_state(r, insert_sort=False):
913	            new_token_ratio = (
914	                1.0 if r.sampling_params.ignore_eos else self.new_token_ratio
915	            )
916	            tokens_left = r.sampling_params.max_new_tokens * new_token_ratio - len(
917	                r.output_ids
918	            )
919	            tokens_occupied = len(r.origin_input_ids) + len(r.output_ids)
920	
921	            if tokens_left <= 0:
922	                return
923	
924	            if not insert_sort:
925	                self.req_states.append((tokens_left, tokens_occupied))
926	            else:
927	                i = 0
928	                for i in range(len(self.req_states)):
929	                    if tokens_left <= self.req_states[i][0]:
930	                        break
931	                self.req_states.insert(i, (tokens_left, tokens_occupied))
932	
933	        if self.req_states is None:
934	            self.req_states = []
935	            add_req_state(req)
936	            if self.running_batch is not None:
937	                for r in self.running_batch.reqs:
938	                    add_req_state(r)
939	            for r in self.can_run_list:
940	                add_req_state(r)
941	            self.req_states.sort(key=lambda x: x[0])
942	        else:
943	            add_req_state(req, insert_sort=True)
944	
945	        if not self.is_hybrid_swa:
946	            # Skip this logic for swa. The SWA has different memory management, and
947	            # this mechanism is underestimating the memory usage.
948	            cur_rem_tokens = self.cur_rem_tokens - self.ceil_paged_tokens(
949	                cand_extend_input_len
950	            )
951	            tokens_freed = 0
952	            for i, (tokens_left, tokens_occupied) in enumerate(self.req_states):
953	                # tokens_left gives a reservative calculation as the last token is not stored
954	                bs = len(self.req_states) - i
955	                min_free_tokens = cur_rem_tokens + tokens_freed - tokens_left * bs
956	                # reserve tokens for corner cases
957	                if min_free_tokens <= IGNORE_EOS_RESERVE_TOKENS * bs:
958	                    return AddReqResult.NO_TOKEN
959	                tokens_freed += tokens_occupied
960	
961	        if (self.prefill_delayer_single_pass is not None) and (
962	            not self.prefill_delayer_single_pass.negotiate_should_allow_prefill(
963	                local_prefillable=True
964	            )
965	        ):
966	            return AddReqResult.OTHER
967	
968	        if self.dllm_config is not None:
969	            if self.rem_dllm_tokens <= 0:
970	                return AddReqResult.OTHER
971	
972	            self._add_dllm_req(req, 0)
973	        elif (
974	            self.rem_chunk_tokens is None  # chunked prefill is disabled
975	            or cand_extend_input_len <= self.rem_chunk_tokens  # it is the last chunk
976	        ):
977	            # Non-chunked prefill — the whole sequence is committed this iter.
978	            req.set_extend_range(
979	                len(req.prefix_indices), len(req.full_untruncated_fill_ids)
980	            )
981	            self.can_run_list.append(req)
982	            self._update_prefill_budget(
983	                0,
984	                req.extend_range.length,
985	                min(req.sampling_params.max_new_tokens, CLIP_MAX_NEW_TOKENS),
986	                req.retracted_stain,
987	                mamba_gap_reserve=self._mamba_gap_budget_for_req(req),
988	            )
989	        else:
990	            if self.rem_chunk_tokens <= 0:
991	                return AddReqResult.OTHER
992	
993	            # Chunked prefill
994	            trunc_len = self.rem_chunk_tokens
995	
996	            assert len(req.prefix_indices) == 0
997	            req.set_extend_range(
998	                len(req.prefix_indices), len(req.prefix_indices) + trunc_len
999	            )
1000	            self.can_run_list.append(req)
1001	            self.new_chunked_req = req
1002	            self._update_prefill_budget(
1003	                0,
1004	                trunc_len,
1005	                0,
1006	                req.retracted_stain,
1007	                mamba_gap_reserve=self._mamba_gap_budget_for_req(req),
1008	            )
1009	
1010	        return self.budget_state()
1011	
1012	    def add_one_req(
1013	        self, req: Req, has_chunked_req: bool, truncation_align_size: Optional[int]
1014	    ):
1015	        # TODO support cp with multiple requests
1016	        # Enabling context parallelism currently presents precision issues;
1017	        # therefore, the prefill-batch setting is temporarily set to 1.
1018	        if (self.dsa_prefill_cp_in_seq_split) and len(self.can_run_list) >= 1:
1019	            return AddReqResult.OTHER
1020	
1021	        if (x := self.prefill_max_requests) is not None and len(self.can_run_list) >= x:
1022	            return AddReqResult.OTHER
1023	
1024	        if req.sampling_params.ignore_eos and getattr(self.tree_cache, "disable", True):
1025	            return self.add_one_req_ignore_eos(req)
1026	
1027	        # Reserve page_size for page-alignment overhead: the paged allocator may
1028	        # consume one extra page per request (see alloc_extend), which
1029	        # _update_prefill_budget also deducts.
1030	        max_new = min(
1031	            max(req.sampling_params.max_new_tokens - len(req.output_ids), 0),
1032	            CLIP_MAX_NEW_TOKENS,
1033	        )
1034	        cand_extend_input_len = len(req.full_untruncated_fill_ids) - len(
1035	            req.prefix_indices
1036	        )
1037	        total_tokens = cand_extend_input_len + max_new + self.page_size
1038	        # Shared Mamba pool: fold the new mamba state's shared-gap cost into
1039	        # `total_tokens` so both `rem_total_tokens` gates reflect the joint budget.
1040	        total_tokens += self._mamba_gap_budget_for_req(req)
1041	
1042	        # adjusting the input_tokens based on host_hit_length and page_size
1043	        real_input_tokens = cand_extend_input_len - req.host_hit_length
1044	        real_input_tokens = self.ceil_paged_tokens(real_input_tokens)
1045	        prefix_len = len(req.prefix_indices)
1046	
1047	        if total_tokens >= self.rem_total_tokens:
1048	            return AddReqResult.NO_TOKEN
1049	
1050	        chunk_tokens_limit = self.rem_chunk_tokens
1051	        if self.is_hybrid_swa:
1052	            # host-hit prefix is loaded back, not re-prefilled, so the SWA peak is
1053	            # driven only by the freshly-prefilled tail (the loaded window is
1054	            # charged separately via swa_host_hit_length).
1055	            swa_needed = self._swa_budget_for_req(
1056	                real_input_tokens, swa_host_hit_length=req.swa_host_hit_length
1057	            )
1058	            if swa_needed >= self.rem_swa_tokens:
1059	                swa_cap = self._swa_chunk_cap(req.swa_host_hit_length)
1060	                if self.rem_chunk_tokens is None or swa_cap <= 0:
1061	                    return AddReqResult.NO_TOKEN
1062	                chunk_tokens_limit = min(self.rem_chunk_tokens, swa_cap)
1063	
1064	        if (
1065	            self.rem_chunk_tokens is None
1066	            and len(self.can_run_list) != 0
1067	            and real_input_tokens >= self.rem_input_tokens
1068	        ):
1069	            # If without chunked prefill:
1070	            # - if the can_run_list is not empty, we satisfy the constraint of (max_prefill_tokens)
1071	            # - if the can_run_list is empty, always accept the first prefill request
1072	            return AddReqResult.OTHER
1073	
1074	        with self._lock_node(req.last_node):
1075	            # self.rem_total_tokens may decrease after the lock acquisition
1076	            if total_tokens >= self.rem_total_tokens:
1077	                return AddReqResult.NO_TOKEN
1078	
1079	            if self.is_hybrid_swa:
1080	                # self.rem_swa_tokens may decrease after the lock acquisition
1081	                swa_needed = self._swa_budget_for_req(
1082	                    real_input_tokens, swa_host_hit_length=req.swa_host_hit_length
1083	                )
1084	                if swa_needed >= self.rem_swa_tokens:
1085	                    swa_cap = self._swa_chunk_cap(req.swa_host_hit_length)
1086	                    if self.rem_chunk_tokens is None or swa_cap <= 0:
1087	                        return AddReqResult.NO_TOKEN
1088	                    chunk_tokens_limit = min(self.rem_chunk_tokens, swa_cap)
1089	
1090	            # Negotiate only after every KV-budget gate (a NO_TOKEN rank must
1091	            # report not-prefillable via finalize()) and before init_load_back
1092	            # (a delay verdict must not start KV load-back).
1093	            if (self.prefill_delayer_single_pass is not None) and (
1094	                not self.prefill_delayer_single_pass.negotiate_should_allow_prefill(
1095	                    local_prefillable=True,
1096	                    running_batch=self.running_batch.batch_size(),
1097	                    max_prefill_bs=self.max_prefill_bs,
1098	                    max_running_requests=self.max_running_requests,
1099	                    waiting_queue_len=self.waiting_queue_len,
1100	                )
1101	            ):
1102	                return AddReqResult.OTHER
1103	
1104	            if req.needs_host_load_back():
1105	                new_indices, req.last_node = self.tree_cache.init_load_back(
1106	                    InitLoadBackParams(
1107	                        best_match_node=req.best_match_node,
1108	                        host_hit_length=req.host_hit_length,
1109	                        req=req,
1110	                    )
1111	                )
1112	                req.prefix_indices = torch.cat([req.prefix_indices, new_indices])
1113	                prefix_len = len(req.prefix_indices)
1114	                req.cache_protected_len = prefix_len
1115	
1116	            input_tokens = self.ceil_paged_tokens(
1117	                len(req.full_untruncated_fill_ids) - len(req.prefix_indices)
1118	            )
1119	
1120	            if (
1121	                self.rem_chunk_tokens is None
1122	                and len(self.can_run_list) != 0
1123	                and input_tokens >= self.rem_input_tokens
1124	            ):
1125	                # If without chunked prefill:
1126	                # - if the can_run_list is not empty, we satisfy the constraint of (max_prefill_tokens)
1127	                # - if the can_run_list is empty, always accept the first prefill request
1128	                return AddReqResult.OTHER
1129	
1130	            if self.dllm_config is not None:
1131	                if self.rem_dllm_tokens <= 0:
1132	                    return AddReqResult.OTHER
1133	
1134	                assert (
1135	                    truncation_align_size is None
1136	                ), "truncation_align_size is not supported for dllm prefill"
1137	
1138	                self._add_dllm_req(req, prefix_len)
1139	                self._req_inc_lock_ref(req)
1140	            elif chunk_tokens_limit is None or input_tokens <= chunk_tokens_limit:
1141	                # Non-chunked prefill — the whole sequence is committed this iter.
1142	                req.set_extend_range(
1143	                    len(req.prefix_indices), len(req.full_untruncated_fill_ids)
1144	                )
1145	                self.can_run_list.append(req)
1146	
1147	                self._req_inc_lock_ref(req)
1148	                self._update_prefill_budget(
1149	                    prefix_len,
1150	                    input_tokens,
1151	                    min(
1152	                        req.sampling_params.max_new_tokens,
1153	                        CLIP_MAX_NEW_TOKENS,
1154	                    ),
1155	                    req.retracted_stain,
1156	                    mamba_gap_reserve=self._mamba_gap_budget_for_req(req),
1157	                )
1158	            else:
1159	                # Make sure at least one page is available
1160	                trunc_len = chunk_tokens_limit // self.page_size * self.page_size
1161	
1162	                if trunc_len <= 0:
1163	                    return AddReqResult.OTHER
1164	
1165	                # When truncation align size is set, we want to assert that the prefill prefix length is multiple of truncation align size
1166	                # A typical use case is when deterministic inference is enabled with flashinfer attention backend,
1167	                # we need the prefill prefix length to be multiple of attention split size
1168	                if truncation_align_size is not None:
1169	                    if trunc_len < truncation_align_size:
1170	                        return AddReqResult.OTHER
1171	                    else:
1172	                        trunc_len = truncation_align_size * (
1173	                            trunc_len // truncation_align_size
1174	                        )
1175	
1176	                now_input_len = trunc_len + len(req.prefix_indices)
1177	                now_input_len = now_input_len // self.page_size * self.page_size
1178	                trunc_len = now_input_len - len(req.prefix_indices)
1179	
1180	                if trunc_len <= 0:
1181	                    return AddReqResult.OTHER
1182	
1183	                # Chunked prefill
1184	                req.set_extend_range(
1185	                    len(req.prefix_indices), len(req.prefix_indices) + trunc_len
1186	                )
1187	
1188	                self.can_run_list.append(req)
1189	                self.new_chunked_req = req
1190	
1191	                self._req_inc_lock_ref(req)
1192	                self._update_prefill_budget(
1193	                    prefix_len,
1194	                    trunc_len,
1195	                    0,
1196	                    req.retracted_stain,
1197	                    mamba_gap_reserve=self._mamba_gap_budget_for_req(req),
1198	                )
1199	
1200	        return self.budget_state()
1201	
1202	    def preempt_to_schedule(self, req: Req, server_args: ServerArgs) -> bool:
1203	        """
1204	        Preempt running requests to serve the new request if the priority threshold is met and token count sum is verified.
1205	        Returns True if preemption was committed, and the new request can be scheduled.
1206	        """
1207	        # Iterate running requests to find preemptible requests
1208	        priority_sign = 1 if server_args.schedule_low_priority_values_first else -1
1209	
1210	        # NOTE: A request finishes in two phases:
1211	        #   1) update_finish_state + release_kv_cache  (in process_batch_result)
1212	        #   2) filter out of batch                (in get_next_batch_to_run / update_running_batch)
1213	        # Preemption runs between these two phases (inside get_new_batch_prefill),
1214	        # so running_batch may still contain requests whose KV cache is already freed.
1215	        # We must skip them here to avoid a double-free on release_req.
1216	        valid_running_reqs = (
1217	            r
1218	            for r in self.running_batch.reqs
1219	            if r not in self.preempt_list and not r.finished()
1220	        )
1221	
1222	        sorted_valid_running_reqs = sorted(
1223	            valid_running_reqs,
1224	            key=lambda x: (
1225	                x.priority * (-priority_sign),
1226	                -x.time_stats.wait_queue_entry_time,
1227	            ),
1228	        )
1229	
1230	        preemptible_reqs = []
1231	        min_tokens_to_remove = (
1232	            len(req.full_untruncated_fill_ids)
1233	            - len(req.prefix_indices)
1234	            + min(req.sampling_params.max_new_tokens, CLIP_MAX_NEW_TOKENS)
1235	            - self.rem_total_tokens
1236	        )
1237	        for running_req in sorted_valid_running_reqs:
1238	            # Priority difference needs to meet the threshold to be preemptible.
1239	            priority_diff = (req.priority - running_req.priority) * (-priority_sign)
1240	
1241	            if priority_diff > self.priority_scheduling_preemption_threshold:
1242	                preemptible_reqs.append(running_req)
1243	                min_tokens_to_remove -= self._get_running_request_total_token_offset(
1244	                    running_req
1245	                )
1246	                if min_tokens_to_remove <= 0:
1247	                    break
1248	            else:
1249	                break
1250	
1251	        # Check max token count limit can be met
1252	        if len(preemptible_reqs) == 0 or min_tokens_to_remove > 0:
1253	            return False
1254	
1255	        # Preempt running requests. Release allocated resources for immediate usage.
1256	        preemptible_reqs = set(preemptible_reqs)
1257	        keep_indices = []
1258	        release_counter = 0
1259	        for i, running_req in enumerate(self.running_batch.reqs):
1260	            if running_req in preemptible_reqs:
1261	                self.rem_total_token_offset -= (
1262	                    self._get_running_request_total_token_offset(running_req)
1263	                )
1264	                release_counter += 1
1265	                self.running_batch.release_req(
1266	                    i, len(self.running_batch.reqs) - release_counter, server_args
1267	                )
1268	            else:
1269	                keep_indices.append(i)
1270	        self.running_batch.filter_batch(keep_indices=keep_indices)
1271	        self.preempt_list.extend(preemptible_reqs)
1272	        return True
